title: iOS 27 Camera App to Get 'Siri' Mode With Nutrition Label Scanning
content:
Apple is planning to integrate Apple Intelligence and Siri into more of its apps in iOS 27, including the Camera app, reports Bloomberg. The ‚ÄåiOS 27‚Äå Camera app will have a dedicated ‚ÄåSiri‚Äå mode that will be available alongside the existing Photo, Video, Portrait, and Panorama modes. When in ‚ÄåSiri‚Äå mode, the existing Camera app shutter button will feature the ‚ÄåApple Intelligence‚Äå logo, letting users know the ‚ÄåSiri‚Äå features are available.


‚ÄåSiri‚Äå mode will incorporate Visual Intelligence, making the feature more accessible. Right now, ‚ÄåVisual Intelligence‚Äå is activated by long pressing the Camera Control button, and it is a gesture that many people may not even be aware of.

In addition to being relocated to the Camera app with ‚ÄåSiri‚Äå branding, ‚ÄåVisual Intelligence‚Äå is also being updated with new features. It will be able to scan a nutrition label on food items to log the dietary information, plus users will be able to use it to add contact details for someone directly to the Contacts app.

MacRumors first discovered signs of the ‚ÄåVisual Intelligence‚Äå features in Apple code in mid-April. Here's a bit more on what we found:

  • Nutrition - Users will be able to scan nutrition labels on food packaging for calorie and macronutrient tracking using the Health app.

  • Contacts - ‚ÄåVisual Intelligence‚Äå will let users scan phone numbers and addresses on business cards and other print media, adding the information to the Contacts app.

  • Wallet - In the Wallet app, ‚ÄåVisual Intelligence‚Äå will capture information from physical event tickets and membership cards, generating digital versions.


Existing ‚ÄåVisual Intelligence‚Äå features will continue to be available, and it will be able to identify objects like plants and animals, add events to the Calendar app, and send visual information to ChatGPT and Google image search. Users will also be able to access the revamped ‚ÄåVisual Intelligence‚Äå through the Camera Control button, but it will open up to the ‚ÄåSiri‚Äå interface in the Camera app instead of the standalone ‚ÄåVisual Intelligence‚Äå experience that we have now.

Apple will introduce ‚ÄåiOS 27‚Äå at the Worldwide Developers Conference that's set to begin on June 8, 2026.
